The learned Ex-Officio Jr-rstice of peace without taking into consideration the report submitted by the police decided the application on 11.6.2015, hence this petition. 3. The purpose of the report/comments from the police has been described in detail in the case titled Khizar Lahore and others". reported as (p.L.D. 2005 Lahore 4iCD in the following terms:- "It is prudent and advisable for an Ex_Officio Justice of the Peace to call for comments of the officer incharge of the relevant police Station in respect of complaints of this nature before taking any decision of his own in that regard so that l.re rnay be apprised of the w.P. The above mentioned dictum clearly indicates importance of the report of the police, so that real facts, should conro ol1 thc lr;colcl, but in tlrc rnul.tcr. in hancl, as statcd abovc:, the learned Ex-Oflicio Justice o1. peace, although has sought report fi'om the police but despite its availability on the recorcl, has ignored it and failed to give any reason for not believing the sane. 6. An Ex-Officio Justice of peace is not bound to seek repoft from the police at every cost and he is firlly colrpetent to decide the application and pass an order even without any report by the police but when a report is called, to know the truth and real facts, as per the above mentioned dictum. then it should not be ignored. IfEx-Officio Justice ofpeace does not agree with the report, then should give the reasons.

An Ex-Officio Justice of peace is not bound to seek repoft from the police at every cost and he is firlly colrpetent to decide the application and pass an order even without any report by the police but when a report is called, to know the truth and real facts, as per the above mentioned dictum. then it should not be ignored. IfEx-Officio Justice ofpeace does not agree with the report, then should give the reasons. Seeking and obtaining a police repofi but ignoring and passing an order, contrary to it, without assigning any reason could not be appreciated. Special care to this situation is required. 7.
